facts: The men's half-marathon race walk final and women's half-marathon race walk final are both scheduled for Thursday, July 27, 2028, starting at 7:30 a.m. PST.[1][2][6]
The session runs from 7:30 a.m. to 11:35 a.m. on July 27, 2028, covering the finals of both men's and women's half-marathon race walk events.[6]
Brian Pintado of Ecuador won the men's 20km race walk at Paris 2024 in 1:18:55.
Caio Bonfim of Brazil took silver in the men's 20km race walk at Paris 2024.
Álvaro Martín of Spain earned bronze in the men's 20km race walk at Paris 2024.
Yang Jiayu of China won gold in the women's 20km race walk at Paris 2024.
María Pérez of Spain took silver in the women's 20km race walk at Paris 2024.
Jemima Montag of Australia earned bronze in the women's 20km race walk at Paris 2024.
The 2028 World Athletics Race Walking Team Championships will feature half-marathon distances and serve as a qualifier for the 2028 Games.[3]
Road events including the half-marathon race walks are set for July 27, 2028.[2]
USA Race Walking lists the 2028 Games half-marathon race walks on July 27, 2028, from 7:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.[4]
World Athletics announced changes from 20km and 35km to half-marathon and marathon race walks for future events including the 2028 Games.[5]

facts: The men's baseball opening round prelim games are scheduled at Dodger Stadium on July 13, 2028, starting at 11:00 AM.
Teams are divided into two three-team groups for a two-game round robin in the opening round from July 13-16.
USA, Dominican Republic, and Venezuela have qualified for the 2028 Games baseball tournament as of the 2026 World Baseball Classic results.
The remaining three teams will qualify through international tournaments in 2027 and 2028.
Tokyo 2020 saw Japan defeat USA 2-0 for gold, with Dominican Republic taking bronze.
Qualification criteria and player eligibility rules for the 2028 Games have not been fully announced.
MLB is optimistic about reaching an agreement for Major League players to participate during an extended All-Star break.

facts: The men's bronze medal game for baseball is scheduled for July 19, 2028, at 2 p.m. EDT at Dodger Stadium.[1]

The baseball tournament at the 2028 Games features six teams in a format with preliminary round robin games from July 13-16, play-in quarterfinals on July 17, semifinals on July 18, and medal games on July 19.[1]
Three teams have qualified for the 2028 Games baseball tournament: United States as host, Dominican Republic, and Venezuela as top finishers from the 2026 World Baseball Classic.[3]
The remaining three spots for the 2028 Games baseball tournament will be determined through international tournaments in 2027 and 2028.[3][4]
Japan won gold at Tokyo 2020 with a 2-0 victory over the United States, while Dominican Republic took bronze.[user-provided background]
Dodger Stadium will host the entire baseball competition from July 13-19, 2028.[1][2]
Qualification criteria and player eligibility rules for the 2028 Games baseball have not been announced as of the latest updates.[1]

facts: The men's gold medal game for baseball is scheduled for July 19, 2028, at 10 p.m. EDT at Dodger Stadium.[1]
The baseball tournament concludes with a doubleheader on July 19 featuring the bronze medal game at 2 p.m. followed by the gold medal game at 10 p.m.[1]
United States, Dominican Republic, and Venezuela have secured spots in the six-team 2028 Games baseball tournament via host status and 2026 World Baseball Classic results.[3]
Three additional teams will qualify for the 2028 Games baseball via 2027 and 2028 international tournaments.[3][4]
Japan defeated United States 2-0 for gold at Tokyo 2020, with both nations likely contenders again based on prior performance.[user-provided background]
The full baseball competition runs July 13-19 at Dodger Stadium, building to semifinals on July 18 and medal games on July 19.[1][2]
Player eligibility rules and full qualification details for the 2028 Games baseball remain unannounced.[1]

facts: The 2028 Summer Games softball tournament features six women's teams competing at OKC Softball Park from July 23-29.
USA has an automatic berth in the 2028 Games softball tournament as the host nation.[1]
Women's preliminary round games are scheduled at OKC Softball Park on July 23.
Japan won gold in softball at Tokyo 2020.
Qualification for the 2028 Games includes one team from the Women's Softball World Cup finals in April 2027.[1]
Three teams will qualify from continental events in 2027 for the 2028 Games softball tournament.[1]
One team will qualify from a final event no later than March 2028.[1]

facts: The men's group stage second round matches occur on July 25 at Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium as part of the T20 format tournament with six teams split into two groups of three initially.[1]

The second phase of the men's competition involves teams playing those from the opposite group not finishing in the same position to determine final standings for medal matches.[1]

No teams have qualified for the men's tournament as of July 2025, with the process not yet finalized.[1]

facts: Remco Evenepoel (BEL) won gold in the Men's Individual Time Trial at Paris 2024 ahead of Filippo Ganna (ITA) silver and Wout van Aert (BEL) bronze.
Grace Brown (AUS) won gold in the Women's Individual Time Trial at Paris 2024 ahead of Anna Henderson (GBR) silver and Chloé Dygert (USA) bronze.
The 2028 Games Men's and Women's Individual Time Trial events are scheduled for Wednesday, July 19, from 10:30 AM to 2:35 PM at a venue listed as TBD.
A maximum of 35 riders may participate in the Men's Individual Time Trial and 35 in the Women's Individual Time Trial at the 2028 Games.
Qualification for the four road cycling events including Men's and Women's Individual Time Trials totals 180 places with 90 per gender.
The Time Trial course for the 2028 Games is set at 35km for both men and women.
The UCI has published qualification systems for cycling events at the 2028 Games, maintaining gender parity with 257 places per gender across all disciplines.

facts: Kristen Faulkner of the USA won gold in the Women's Road Race at Paris 2024.
Marianne Vos of the NED won silver in the Women's Road Race at Paris 2024.
Lotte Kopecky of the BEL won bronze in the Women's Road Race at Paris 2024.
The Women's Road Race at the 2028 Games will start at the Venice Beach Boardwalk.
The Women's Road Race course at the 2028 Games covers 150km.
A total of 90 qualification places are allocated for women's road cycling events including the road race at the 2028 Games.
A maximum of 4 athletes per NOC can be entered in the women's road race at the 2028 Games.
Athletes must have at least 10 UCI points in the Individual UCI Road World Ranking of 19 October 2027 or 13 June 2028 to be eligible for the 2028 Games road events.
The women's road race route at the 2028 Games is 163 km with 2,950 meters of elevation gain.
The women's road race is scheduled for Saturday July 22 from 11:30 AM to 4:15 PM at the Venice Beach Boardwalk.

facts: Canoe Slalom men's and women's kayak single heats, including 1st and 2nd runs, are scheduled at OKC Whitewater Center on July 14, 2028, coinciding with the opening ceremony day.[1][3]
OKC Whitewater Center will host all Canoe Slalom events from July 14 to 22, 2028, and is set to host the ICF Canoe Slalom World Championships in 2027.[1]
The venue hosted a canoe slalom final at Riversport on March 29, 2026, providing a preview for 2028 events.[5]
Paris 2024 women's kayak single gold medalist Jessica Fox of Australia leads the world rankings and is expected to contend based on recent performances.
Paris 2024 men's kayak single gold medalist Giovanni de Gennaro of Italy retired after Paris but has not confirmed plans for 2028 as of April 2026.
Top contenders for women's K1 include Paris 2024 silver medalist Elena Lantsova of Belarus and bronze medalist Valeria Merkacheva of Kazakhstan.

USA is expected to contend in the men's tournament based on international rankings.[3]
Mexico leads the women's world rankings per the International Federation of American Football.[3]
Great Britain, Japan, and Canada are projected contenders for the women's tournament.[3]
Qualification for the 2028 Games includes a last-chance Olympic Qualifier Series tournament with 10 men's and 10 women's teams.[5]

India secured bronze in men's field hockey at Paris 2024 with a penalty shootout win over Spain.
The 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games men's field hockey tournament features 12 teams in a preliminary pool stage at Carson Stadium.
Teams play five pool matches each in the preliminary round, with the top four from each pool advancing to quarterfinals.
The United States received a direct quota place in the men's tournament as the host nation.
FIH Olympic Qualification Tournaments for the remaining spots are scheduled for early 2028.

facts: The men's qualifying round on July 24 at Exposition Park Stadium features three games in the preliminary stage of the 2028 Summer Games lacrosse sixes tournament.
Six men's teams will compete in the 2028 Games after qualification through continental championships in 2026, World Lacrosse Sixes Championships in 2027, and a final tournament in early 2028.
The United States men's team automatically qualifies as host nation if participating in continental and world championships.

Continental championships for men's sixes lacrosse are scheduled from September to December 2026, including Pan-American in Oshawa, Canada (September 29-October 4), Asia-Pacific in Sunshine Coast, Australia (October 5-10), European in Salou, Spain (November 2-18), and African TBD.
The 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships will allocate four Olympic spots to top teams plus the host if eligible, with teams needing top-12 finish for further qualification eligibility.
Lacrosse sixes uses a 70x36 meter field, four 8-minute quarters, 30-second shot clock, and 12-player rosters where all players handle offense and defense.

facts: The men's bronze and gold medal games for lacrosse sixes will take place at Exposition Park Stadium on July 29.[3]

Six men's teams will compete in the 2028 Games lacrosse sixes tournament, with the United States automatically qualifying as host nation if participating in continental championships and World Lacrosse Sixes Championships.[3]

Continental championships for men's lacrosse sixes are scheduled from September to December 2026 in Pan-American (Oshawa, Canada, Sep 29-Oct 4), Asia-Pacific (Sunshine Coast, Australia, Oct 5-10), European (Salou, Spain, Nov 2-18), and African (TBD, expected Dec) locations.[3]

The 2027 World Lacrosse Sixes Championships will qualify four men's teams plus the host United States for the 2028 Games, with top 12 finishers eligible and limits on teams per continental federation.[3]
A final qualifying tournament in March or April 2028 will determine the last men's spot among the top six eligible teams based on 2027 championships rankings.[3]
Lacrosse sixes features teams of six players on fields approximately 70 yards long and 40 yards wide, with four 8-minute quarters and a 30-second shot clock.[1][2]

facts: Belmont Shore in Long Beach serves as the venue for all 2028 Summer Games coastal rowing sessions, including women's and men's solo time trials and last 16 rounds[1][3].
Coastal rowing beach sprints debut at the 2028 Summer Games with solo and double sculls events in men's, women's, and mixed categories[1][2].
The International Olympic Committee confirmed beach sprint rowing for the 2028 Summer Games program on April 9, 2025[1].
Rowing maintains its athlete quota while adding three beach sprint medal events to the 12 classic rowing events for a total of 15[1].
Coastal rowing boats for beach sprints are singles and doubles designed for navigating tides, currents, rough waters, and marine traffic[2].
No Paris 2024 medal results exist for coastal rowing as the discipline was not contested.
No qualification statuses or roster announcements for 2028 Summer Games coastal rowing solo events have been reported as of April 2026.
No world rankings or recent 2024-2026 results specific to coastal rowing solo time trials or last 16 rounds are available from current sources.

facts: The men's Rugby Sevens semifinals, 5th-8th place semifinals, and 9th-12th place finals are scheduled for July 18, 2028, from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM at Carson Stadium.[2]
Carson Stadium is the venue for the men's Rugby Sevens knockout stages, including semifinals and placement matches.[1][2]
Teams advancing to the semifinals on July 18 will be the top four from the men's pool play and quarterfinals held on July 16-17.[2]
The 5th-8th place semifinals and 9th-12th place finals occur concurrently with the main semifinals in this session.[2]
New Zealand won gold in the men's Rugby Sevens at Paris 2024.
Argentina won silver in the men's Rugby Sevens at Paris 2024.
France won bronze in the men's Rugby Sevens at Paris 2024.
Fiji finished fifth in the men's Rugby Sevens at Paris 2024.
The HSBC SVNS Series features eight core men's teams in Division 1, including New Zealand, Argentina, and Fiji, competing across six events leading to World Championships.[3]
World Rugby's evolved SVNS model includes promotion and relegation, affecting qualification pathways for the 2028 Games.[3][6]

facts: Belmont Shore serves as the venue for windsurfing and kite events in the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games.[3]
Kite events follow a format with multi-day qualifiers leading to finals.[2]
The men's Formula Kite event at Paris 2024 was won by Valentin Bontus of Austria, with Toni Vodišek of Slovenia taking silver and Maximilian Maeder of Singapore bronze.
The women's Formula Kite event at Paris 2024 was won by Lauriane Nolot of France, with Julia Karla Moura Santos of Brazil taking silver and Jingle Chen of China bronze.
No qualification spots have been confirmed for kite events as of April 2026.
Testing of new formats for kiteboarding is planned ahead of the 2028 Games.[1]

facts: Yuto Horigome of Japan won gold in Men's Street at Paris 2024.
Jagger Eaton of USA won silver in Men's Street at Paris 2024.
Nyjah Huston of USA won bronze in Men's Street at Paris 2024.
World Skateboarding qualification for the 2028 Games consists of Phase 1 from June 11, 2026, to March 31, 2028, via the World Skateboarding Tour, which is the sole funnel to Phase 2[1][2].
The top 44 athletes per gender and discipline from Phase 1 World Skateboarding Rankings advance to Phase 2 from April 1 to June 11, 2028, with a maximum of six athletes per country[1][2][3].

At the end of Phase 2, 22 athletes per event qualify for the 2028 Games, with a maximum of three per nation, at least one per continent, at least one from the host country if not already qualified, and one universality spot[1][2][3].
Each national team can register up to three athletes per World Skateboarding Tour event, with up to six additional per nation via top 30 World Skateboarding Ranking 60 days prior[1][2].
World Skateboarding Rankings consider best results from the prior 18 months of World Skateboarding Tour events[1].
The Men's Street Final is scheduled at Valley Complex 1 on Thursday, July 20, from 8:30 PM to 10:00 PM[5].
Phase 1 of qualification begins with the WST Rome World Cup from June 7-21, 2026[1].

facts: Tennis at the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games features men's singles, women's singles, men's doubles, women's doubles, and mixed doubles with a total of 172 athletes.[1]
Each singles draw consists of 64 players, starting with Round 1 matches.[1]
Qualification for singles is primarily based on ATP and WTA singles rankings as of June 12, 2028, with 56 direct acceptances per gender.[1]
A maximum of four athletes per NOC can enter the singles draw.[1]
The tennis competition is scheduled between July 14 and 30, 2028.[1]

facts: Cassandre Beaugrand of France won gold in the women's individual triathlon at Paris 2024.
Julie Derron of Switzerland won silver in the women's individual triathlon at Paris 2024.
Beth Potter of Great Britain won bronze in the women's individual triathlon at Paris 2024.
The women's individual triathlon final for the 2028 Games is scheduled at Venice Beach.
Triathlon events at the 2028 Games were relocated from Long Beach to Venice Beach.[1][3][4]
The 2028 Games triathlon program includes the women's individual as one of three medal events.[1][3]
Athlete quota for women's triathlon at the 2028 Games remains 55, same as Paris 2024.[1][3]

facts: Beach volleyball at the 2028 Games will feature 24 men's teams and 24 women's teams qualifying through continental championships in 2026, world championships in 2027, Olympic ranking closing in 2028, and Olympic Qualifier Series.[1]
USA receives one guaranteed quota place per gender as host nation and can pursue a second through standard pathways.[1]
Sweden won gold in men's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Germany won silver in men's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Norway won bronze in men's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Brazil 1 won gold in women's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Canada 2 won silver in women's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Switzerland 2 won bronze in women's beach volleyball at Paris 2024.
Alamitos Beach Stadium in Long Beach will host all beach volleyball sessions from July 15 to 29.[1]

facts: Italy won gold in women's volleyball at Paris 2024.
United States won silver in women's volleyball at Paris 2024.
Brazil won bronze in women's volleyball at Paris 2024.
All women's volleyball medal matches for the 2028 Games, including the bronze medal match, will take place at the Honda Center between July 28 and 30.[1]
The women's bronze medal match is scheduled for July 29 at the Honda Center.[1]

Qualification for the 2028 Games women's volleyball tournament includes 5 continental champions, 3 teams from the World Championships, 3 teams from the world ranking, and 1 spot for the host country USA.[1]
The largest share of quota places for the 2028 Games will be allocated through the Olympic Ranking List closing in 2028, with the top 14 teams per gender securing a quota.[2]
The Olympic Qualifier Series will offer 3 quota places per gender for the 2028 Games, with details to be announced in 2026.[2]

Croatia won the silver medal in men's water polo at Paris 2024.
United States won the bronze medal in men's water polo at Paris 2024.

Men's semifinals precede the bronze medal match on July 21.
